AAFLIPFLOP.description: The AAFLIPFLOP algorithm simulates a memory device whose output state is inverted by each momentary TRUE signal on SRST (that is, the output state OUT is inverted when there is a transition in the input SRST from a FALSE to a TRUE signal).

ABSVALUE.description: The output for the ABSVALUE algorithm is the absolute value of input IN1.

ALARMMON.description: The ALARMMON algorithm sets OUT equal to TRUE if any of the inputs are in alarm (alarm status is TRUE).

ANALOG.description: The Analog Output Device algorithm is used to interface to local analog loop Controllers.

ports.name: INC
ports.description: Input (digital signal to increase the step number)

ports.name: DEC
ports.description: Input (digital signal to decrease the step number)

ports.name: TRIN
ports.description: Input (analog); tracks the step number to this value

ports.name: R16
ports.description: Output value for Step 16 (+ or -) or Output value 2 for Step 1

ports.name: R17
ports.description: Output value for Step 17 (+ or -) or Output value 2 for Step 2

ports.name: R18
ports.description: Output value for Step 18 (+ or -) or Output value 2 for Step 3

ports.name: R19
ports.description: Output value for Step 19 (+ or -) or Output value 2 for Step 4

ports.name: R20
ports.description: Output value for Step 20 (+ or -) or Output value 2 for Step 5

ports.name: R21
ports.description: Output value for Step 21 (+ or -) or Output value 2 for Step 6

ports.name: R22
ports.description: Output value for Step 22 (+ or -) or Output value 2 for Step 7

ports.name: R23
ports.description: Output value for Step 23 (+ or -) or Output value 2 for Step 8

ports.name: R24
ports.description: Output value for Step 24 (+ or -) or Output value 2 for Step 9

ports.name: R25
ports.description: Output value for Step 25 (+ or -) or Output value 2 for Step 10

ports.name: R26
ports.description: Output value for Step 26 (+ or -) or Output value 2 for Step 11

ports.name: R27
ports.description: Output value for Step 27 (+ or -) or Output value 2 for Step 12

ports.name: R28
ports.description: Output value for Step 28 (+ or -) or Output value 2 for Step 13

ports.name: R29
ports.description: Output value for Step 29 (+ or -) or Output value 2 for Step 14

ports.name: R30
ports.description: Output value for Step 30(+ or -) or Output value 2 for Step 15

ports.name: STEP
ports.description: Output (analog); first selected by the current step number

ports.name: OUT
ports.description: Output (analog); selected by the current step number

ports.name: OUT2
ports.description: Output (analog); second selected by the current step number

ANALOGDRUM.description: The ANALOGDRUM algorithm is a software drum controller with one analog output value and up to 30 steps OR a software drum controller with two analog output values and up to 15 steps.

AND.description: The AND algorithm is a logical AND gate that can implement up to eight inputs.

ANNUNCIATOR.description: The ANNUNCIATOR algorithm calculates one alarm window state of a lamp box, based on the result of annunciator logic (IN1), the previous window state (OUT) and the inputs from the operator push-button interface (ACK, RSET, TEST).

ports.name: OUT
ports.description: Output (analog); antilog of the input value

ANTILOG.description: The ANTILOG algorithm scales an input value and outputs the antilog of the scaled value.
ANTILOG.formula: OUT = e (IN1 x SCAL) if BASE = 0 OR OUT = 10(IN1 x SCAL) if BASE = 1 Note For range-checking purposes, the upper and lower limits of x such that ex can be represented as a real number are: HIGH = 88.72 (HIGH is less than IN * (3.4E + 38) = 88.722) LOW = -87.31 (LOW is greater than IN * (4.4E - 38) = -87.316)

ARCCOSINE.description: The ARCCOSINE algorithm performs the mathematical arc cosine function.

ARCSINE.description: The ARCSINE algorithm performs the mathematical arc sine function.

ARCTANGENT.description: The ARCTANGENT algorithm performs the mathematical arc tangent function.

ASSIGN.description: The ASSIGN algorithm transfers point value and the quality of one point to another point of the same record type.

ATREND.description: The ATREND algorithm outputs a user-specified point to a strip chart recorder for trending.

AVALGEN.description: The AVALGEN algorithm initializes an analog point.

ports.name: CNTL
ports.description: Type of control to be implemented: NORMAL:Normal control mode BALANCER:MA Balancer control mode

ports.name: TRK
ports.description: Type of tracking to be implemented: HIGHEST: Highest value LOWEST: Lowest value AVERAGE: Average value

ports.name: GAIN
ports.description: Gain on input variable. The gain on the input should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: TPSC
ports.description: Maximum value of the output point

ports.name: BTSC
ports.description: Minimum value of the output point

ports.name: TOUT
ports.description: Upstream input track value, mode and status of the algorithm

ports.name: TRK01
ports.description: One feedback tracking signal from a downstream algorithm which consists of tracking value and tracking signals (in the third status field of analog track point)

ports.name: TRK02
ports.description: One feedback tracking signal from a downstream algorithm which consists of tracking value and tracking signals (in the third status field of analog track point)

ports.name: TRK03
ports.description: One feedback tracking signals from a downstream algorithm which consists of tracking value and tracking signals (in the third status field of analog track point)

BALANCER.description: The BALANCER algorithm monitors the modes of up to 16 downstream algorithms and performs a user-defined type of tracking when all of the downstream algorithms are requesting the upstream algorithm to track.

ports.name: BITP
ports.description: Bit position of the first digit

ports.name: NDIG
ports.description: Number of digits to read: 1, 2, 3, or 4

ports.name: CNTL
ports.description: Control Action Indicator: Value Description 0 Direct Action - Card value is read from the I/O Bus and used. This is for a BCD display where low is TRUE. 1 Inverse Action - Card value is read from the I/O Bus, inverted and then used. This is for a BCD display where high is TRUE.

BCDNIN.description: The BCDNIN algorithm reads a packed point (IN) in Binary Coded Decimal (BCD), converts it to a real number, and stores it in the AV field of the output record (OUT).

ports.name: BITP
ports.description: Bit position of the first digit

ports.name: NDIG
ports.description: Number of digits to read: 1, 2, 3, or 4

ports.name: CNTL
ports.description: Control Action Indicator: Value Description 0 DirectAction-Cardvalueis read from the hardware and used. This is for a BCD display where low is TRUE. 1 Inverse Action - Card value is read from the hardware, inverted and then used. This is for a BCD display where high is TRUE.

BCDNOUT.description: The BCDNOUT algorithm reads a real value from input IN, converts it to binary-coded-decimal (BCD), and outputs a number of digits to a packed output point.

ports.name: FLOW
ports.description: stream Flow Element Type (Downstream or Upstream)

BILLFLOW.description: The BILLFLOW algorithm generates an AGA3 gas flow calculation for orifices.

C5.description: Bit 0 Emergency Close/Stop Bit 1 Emergency Open/Start Bit 6 Current State of Input 1 (Open/Start) Bit 7 Current State of Input 2 (Close/Stop) Bit 8 Current State of Input 3 (Stop) Bit 9 Value currently being written to Output 1 (Open/Start) Bit 10 Value currently being written to Output 2 (Close/Stop) Bit 11 Value currently being written to Output 3 (Stop) Bit 12 Close/Stop permissive Bit 13 Open/Start permissive Table 3-3.

CALCBLOCK.description: The CALCBLOCK algorithm is designed to allow the user to solve complex mathematical equations within a control sheet.

CALCBLOCKD.description: The CALCBLOCKD algorithm is the digital version of the CALCBLOCK algorithm (see Section 3-21).

COMPARE.description: The COMPARE algorithm will compare the value of IN1 as compared with the value of the IN2, and the appropriate output is set TRUE.

COSINE.description: The COSINE algorithm performs the mathematical cosine function.

COUNTER.description: The COUNTER algorithm will count up or down based on the input DIRECTION for the direction to count.

DBEQUALS.description: The DBEQUALS high/low comparator algorithm monitors two analog input values.
DBEQUALS.formula: TEMP = IN1 - IN2 IF ABS(TEMP) > DBAN THEN OUT = TRUE IF OUT = TRUE THEN IF ABS(TEMP) <ABS(DBN-RTRN) THEN OUT = FALSE where:

ports.name: MSTR
ports.description: Status point for communication with MASTERSEQ

ports.name: FAIL
ports.description: Input from device Logic functions indicating that a failure has occurred.

ports.name: PASS
ports.description: Input from device Logic functions indicating that the execution of the device has completed successfully.

ports.name: RDY
ports.description: Input from device Logic functions indicating that the associated device is ready for a start command.

ports.name: TARG
ports.description: Delay Time. Defines the amount of time that can elapse, while the device is running, before the DEVICESEQ algorithm will set the failure bit in the status point to Logic Delay Time 1. If the value of the parameters is 0.0 the internal failure timer will be disabled.

ports.name: ACT
ports.description: Output value of internal failure timer.

ports.name: TIME
ports.description: Output value of step timer. Represents the amount of time the device has been running

DEVICESEQ.description: The DEVICESEQ algorithm provides an interface between control logic function and a MASTERSEQ algorithm.

ports.name: SET
ports.description: Set time-out (Max of 255 if units are 1/ 10 seconds or seconds. Max. of 100 if units are minutes.)

ports.name: RSET
ports.description: Reset time-out (Max of 255 if units are 1/10 seconds or seconds. Max. of 100 if units are minutes.)

ports.name: TU
ports.description: Units time-out Value Time Units 0 - Tenths of Seconds 1 - Seconds 2 - Minutes

DEVICEX.description: The DEVICEX algorithm combines the commands to open and close or start and stop a piece of equipment with feedback signals indicating the command was accomplished.

DIGCOUNT.description: The DIGCOUNT algorithm sets the output digital FLAG TRUE if M inputs or more of the N digital inputs are TRUE (where N ≤ 12, and M and N are constants).

ports.name: INC
ports.description: Input (digital signal to increase the step number)

ports.name: DEC
ports.description: Input (digital signal to decrease the step number)

ports.name: TRIN
ports.description: Input (analog); tracks the step number to this value

ports.name: TYPE
ports.description: Steps are Long or Short. Long = Step 1 to Step 50 defines up to 32 outputs. Short = Double number of steps to 100 since parameter “I/01” contains Step 1 value in the lower 16 bits, and Step 2 value in the higher 16 bits. Therefore, only up to 16 outputs are used.

ports.name: O01
ports.description: Output (digital signal); least-significant bit; set or reset according to the bits in the integer selected by the current step number

ports.name: O31
ports.description: Output (digital signal); set or reset according to the bits in the integer selected by the current step number

ports.name: O32
ports.description: Output (digital signal); most significant bit set or reset according to the bits in the integer selected by the current step number

DIGDRUM.description: The DIGDRUM algorithm is a software drum controller with up to 32 digital output values and up to 50 steps.

ports.name: SPRI
ports.description: Sampler Reset Priority 0 = Operator Keyboard ON 1 = Operator Keyboard OFF

ports.name: BASE
ports.description: Time base for total run 0 = Hours 1 = Minutes 2 = Seconds

ports.name: BASE
ports.description: Time base for total run. 0 = Hours 1 = Minutes 2 = Seconds

ports.name: BASE
ports.description: Time for base for total run. 0 = Hours 1 = Minutes 2 = Seconds

ports.name: BASE
ports.description: Time base for total run: 0 = Hours 1 = Minutes 2 = Seconds

ports.name: BASE
ports.description: Time base for total run. 0 = Hours 1 = Minutes 2 = Seconds

ports.name: DLY1
ports.description: Time for Close (should be greater than loop time)

ports.name: DLY2
ports.description: Time for Open (should be greater than loop time)

ports.name: BASE
ports.description: Time base for total run 0 = Hours 1 = Minutes 2 = Seconds

DIGITAL.description: The Digital Device algorithm provides logic to control the following seven types of devices: • SAMPLER (Controlled Sampler) (Section 3-31.1).

ports.name: TPSC
ports.description: Maximum value of the output point

ports.name: BTSC
ports.description: Minimum value of the output point

ports.name: TOUT
ports.description: Track output value, mode & status for input variable

ports.name: TRIN
ports.description: Tracking analog value, tracking and limiting mode signals input variance

DIVIDE.description: The DIVIDE algorithm divides two gained and biased inputs.

ports.name: AOUT
ports.description: Output (analog); contents of the DU record field.

ports.name: POUT
ports.description: Output (packed); contents of the DU record field.

DROPSTATUS.description: The DROPSTATUS algorithm accesses and outputs the contents of any record field in the Drop Status Record (DU) for a particular Controller.

ports.name: MNIN
ports.description: Maximum number of steps per rod

ports.name: BOTS
ports.description: Number of Bottom Coils for Shutdown Rod

ports.name: IN1
ports.description: Input (packed) Bits 0 - 4 - Gray Bit 5 - Gray Code A Error Bit Bits 6 - 10 - Gray Code B Bit 11 - Gray Code B Error Bit Bit 12 - CD24 Monitor. Indicator that zero value can be accepted. Bit 13 - Rapid Refuel in progress

DRPI.description: The DRPI algorithm converts the gray codes (IN1) to actual rod positions.

ports.name: VALU
ports.description: Real value (0.0 = FALSE; 1.0 or any other non-zero, real number = TRUE)

DVALGEN.description: The DVALGEN algorithm initializes a digital point.

ports.name: TOUT
ports.description: Track Output Value Mode and Status For Input Variable (analog)

FIELD.description: The FIELD algorithm is used only with the hardware analog output variable points.

FIFO.description: The FIFO algorithm provides a basic First In - First Out operation.

FLIPFLOP.description: The FLIPFLOP algorithm is a memory device.

ports.name: GAIN
ports.description: Gain on the input. The gain on the input should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm

ports.name: TPSC
ports.description: Maximum value of the output point

ports.name: BTSC
ports.description: Minimum value of the output point

ports.name: BPTS
ports.description: Number of break points (number of (x, y) coordinate pairs)

ports.name: TOUT
ports.description: Track output value, mode and status signals for input variable

ports.name: TRIN
ports.description: Tracking analog value, tracking and limiting mode signals input variance

FUNCTION.description: The FUNCTION algorithm generates a piecewise-linear function that is determined by elements of a 12-element X-Y breakpoint array.

ports.name: GAIN
ports.description: Gain on input. The gain on the input should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: TPSC
ports.description: Maximum value of the output point

ports.name: BTSC
ports.description: Minimum value of the output point

ports.name: PROQ
ports.description: Quality Propagation option: ON=Normal quality selection OFF=Quality of the output is always GOOD, except when OUTPUT is not a valid real number.

ports.name: TOUT
ports.description: Track output value, mode and status signals for input variable

ports.name: TRIN
ports.description: Tracking analog value, tracking and limiting mode signals input variable

GAINBIAS.description: The GAINBIAS algorithm multiplies the analog input with an internal gain, adds a bias and then limits the output value.
GAINBIAS.formula: OUT = (IN1 x GAIN) + BIAS IF OUT ≥ TPSC THEN OUT = TPSC ELSE IF OUT ≤ BTSC THEN OUT = BTSC

ports.name: GAIN
ports.description: Calculated flow coefficient at base operating conditions.

ports.name: PRES
ports.description: Reference pressure; base operating pressure used in the calculation of the flow coefficient, GAIN

ports.name: TEMP
ports.description: Reference temperature; base operating temperature used in the calculation of the flow coefficient, GAIN.

ports.name: TOUT
ports.description: Track output value and status output signals

ports.name: TRIN
ports.description: Track value, tracking and limit modes. Analog input

GASFLOW.description: The GASFLOW algorithm calculates a pressure-and-temperature-compensated mass or volumetric flow for ideal gases.

HIGHLOWMON.description: The HIGHLOWMON algorithm is a high and low signal monitor with reset deadband and fixed/variable limits.

ports.name: HISP
ports.description: Set point for the high signal monitor trip point

HIGHMON.description: The HIGHMON algorithm is a high signal monitor with reset deadband and a fixed/ variable limit.
HIGHMON.formula: IF IN1 > HISP THEN OUT = TRUE ELSE IF IN1 < (HISP - HIDB) THEN OUT = FALSE

ports.name: IN1G
ports.description: Gain on input 1. The gain on the input1 should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: IN2G
ports.description: Gain on input 2. The gain on the input 2 should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: IN3G
ports.description: Gain on input 3. The gain on the input 3 should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm

ports.name: IN4G
ports.description: Gain on input 4, the gain on the input 4 should never be initialized to zero; if it is the drop is placed into alarm

ports.name: TPSC
ports.description: Maximum value of the output point

ports.name: BTSC
ports.description: Minimum value of the output point

ports.name: QUAL
ports.description: Quality Definition WORSE Worst quality of the four inputs is selected. SELECTED Output point is assigned the quality of the selected input point.Ifthevaluesoftheinput points are equal, the best quality is selected.

ports.name: TRK1
ports.description: Track output value mode and status signals for Input 1 variable

ports.name: TRK2
ports.description: Track output value mode status signals for Input 2 variable

ports.name: TRK3
ports.description: Track output value mode signal for Input 3

ports.name: TRK4
ports.description: Track output value mode signal for Input 4

ports.name: TRIN
ports.description: Tracking and signals analog input variable

HISELECT.description: The HISELECT algorithm performs a gain and bias on the four inputs.

HSCLTP.description: HSCLTP calculates Enthalpy (H) and Entropy (S) of Compressed Liquid given its Temperature and Pressure.

HSLT.description: HSLT calculates Enthalpy (H) of Saturated Liquid given its Temperature.

HSTVSVP.description: HSTVSVP calculates Enthalpy (H), Entropy (S), Temperature, and Specific Volume of Saturated Vapor given its Pressure.

HSVSSTP.description: HSVSSTP calculates Enthalpy (H), Entropy (S), and Specific Volume of Superheated Steam given its Temperature and Pressure.

ports.name: TYPE
ports.description: Interpolation Type. Currently only linear is supported.

ports.name: VALID
ports.description: Equals Logic 1 when the XIN value is found to fall between two X values in the table (digital).

INTERP.description: The INTERP algorithm provides a linear table-lookup and interpolation function.

ports.name: LENG
ports.description: Length of output pulse in loops.

ports.name: PK1
ports.description: Output (digital); passed on from Function Key F1 or Programmable Key P1.

ports.name: PK2
ports.description: Output (digital); passed on from Function Key F2 or Programmable Key P2.

ports.name: PK3
ports.description: Output (digital); passed on from Function Key F3 or Programmable Key P3.

ports.name: PK4
ports.description: Output (digital); passed on from Function Key F4 or Programmable Key P4.

ports.name: PK5
ports.description: Output (digital); passed on from Function Key F5 or Programmable Key P5.

ports.name: PK6
ports.description: Output (digital); passed on from Function Key F6 or Programmable Key P6.

ports.name: PK7
ports.description: Output (digital); passed on from Function Key F7 or Programmable Key P7.

ports.name: PK8
ports.description: Output (digital); passed on from Function Key F8 or Programmable Key P8.

ports.name: OPEN
ports.description: Output (digital); Passed on from KEYBOARD START/OPEN

ports.name: CLOS
ports.description: Output (digital): Passed on from KEYBOARD STOP/CLOSE

ports.name: SPUP
ports.description: Output (digital): Passed on from KEYBOARD set point INCREASE (↑)

ports.name: SPDN
ports.description: Output (digital); Passed on from KEYBOARD set point Decrease (↓)

ports.name: AUTO
ports.description: Output (digital); Passed on from KEYBOARD AUTO

ports.name: MAN
ports.description: Output (digital); Passed on from KEYBOARD MANUAL

ports.name: INC
ports.description: Output (digital); Passed on from KEYBOARD Output Increase (∆)

ports.name: DEC
ports.description: Output (digital); Passed on from KEYBOARD Output Decrease (∇)

KEYBOARD.description: The KEYBOARD (Key Interface) algorithm interfaces the ten control keys (start/open, stop/close, auto, man, ↑, ↓, ∆, ∇) to the Controller in the most basic form.

ports.name: SET
ports.description: Digital input. Set Latched Quality bit flag.

ports.name: QUAL
ports.description: Packed input. Indicates the quality to which IN1 is to be set.

ports.name: RSET
ports.description: Digital input. Clear Latched Quality bit flag.

LATCHQUAL.description: The LATCHQUAL algorithm latches and unlatches the quality of an input analog or digital point.

ports.name: IN1G
ports.description: Gain on input. The gain on the input should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm

ports.name: TPSC
ports.description: Maximum value of the output point

ports.name: BTSC
ports.description: Minimum value of the output point

ports.name: LAG
ports.description: Lag time constant (second) Note: This is approximately 1/5 of the total time to settle. For example, for a 1 minute total, set LAG to 12 seconds.

ports.name: TOUT
ports.description: Track output value, for mode & status signals for Input 1 variable

ports.name: TRIN
ports.description: Tracking & Limiting mode signals and tracking value; analog input variable

LEADLAG.description: LEADLAG is a nonlinear lead/lag function.

ports.name: TEMP
ports.description: Temperature of water in reference leg (F)

LEVELCOMP.description: The LEVELCOMP algorithm calculates the density compensated water level in a pressurized steam drum.

LOG.description: The LOG algorithm performs the mathematical logarithmic function.

ports.name: IN1G
ports.description: Gain on Input 1. The gain on the input should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: IN2G
ports.description: Gain on input 2. The gain on the input should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: IN3G
ports.description: Gain on Input 3. The gain on the input should never be initialized to zero.

ports.name: IN4G
ports.description: Gain on Input 4. The gain on the input should never be initialized to zero.

ports.name: TPSC
ports.description: Maximum value of the output point.

ports.name: BTSC
ports.description: Minimum value of the output point.

ports.name: QUAL
ports.description: Quality Definition WORSE Worst quality of the four inputs is selected. SELECTED Output point is assigned the quality of the selected input point. If the values of the inputpointsareequal,thebest quality is selected.

ports.name: TRK1
ports.description: Track output value mode & status signals for Input 1

ports.name: TRK2
ports.description: Track output value mode & status signals for input 2 variable

ports.name: TRIN
ports.description: Tracking & Limiting mode signals and tracking value analog input variable

LOSELECT.description: The LOSELECT algorithm performs a gain and bias on the four inputs.

ports.name: LOSP
ports.description: Set point for the low signal monitor trip point

LOWMON.description: The LOWMON algorithm is a low signal monitor with reset deadband and a fixed variable limit.
LOWMON.formula: IF IN1 < LOSP THEN OUT = TRUE ELSE IF IN1 > (LOSP + LODB) THEN OUT = FALSE

ports.name: TRK
ports.description: MASTATION is based on TRIN point

MAMODE.description: The MAMODE is used in conjunction with a MASTATION algorithm.

ports.name: IN1G
ports.description: Gain on input. The cascade gain should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: TPSC
ports.description: Maximum value of the output point

ports.name: BTSC
ports.description: Minimum value of the output point

ports.name: TPBS
ports.description: Maximum value of the bias bar

ports.name: BTBS
ports.description: Minimum value of the bias bar

ports.name: PCNT
ports.description: Percent change in first four seconds

ports.name: TIME
ports.description: Number of seconds remaining for ramp to full scale

ports.name: FP
ports.default: MANUAL First pass mode. Algorithm goes to this
ports.description: mode on reset/power-up: MANUAL: Manual mode AUTO: Auto mode

ports.name: TYPE
ports.description: Interface Card type: SOFT: Soft M/A only RLI:LI card RVP:VP Card

ports.name: REJQ
ports.description: Quality reject type (only has meaning if Manual Inhibit is OFF): BAD:Algorithm rejects to Manual mode whenthequalityoftheIN1inputgoesBAD and the algorithm is in Auto mode. NOTGOOD:Algorithm rejects to Manual mode when the quality of the IN1 input is NOT GOOD and the algorithm is in Auto mode. OFF:The quality of the IN1 input is not checked or used to reject the algorithm to Manual mode when the algorithm is in Auto mode.

ports.name: CNFG
ports.description: NORMAL:Upstream algorithm is not BALANCER BALANCER:Upstream algorithm is

ports.name: TOUT
ports.description: Track output value mode & status signals for Input 1 variable

ports.name: MODE
ports.description: Output point from the MAMODE algorithm

ports.name: TRIN
ports.description: Tracking & Limiting mode signals & tracking value; analog Input variable

MASTATION.description: MASTATION algorithm interfaces a CRT-based soft manual/auto station and an optional Ovation Loop Interface module card with the functional processor.

ports.name: ENBL
ports.description: MASTERSEQ is Inoperative anytime this signal is FALSE.

ports.name: OVRD
ports.description: Proceed to Next Step ignoring any status of the current Step.

ports.name: FAIL
ports.description: TRUE When a DEVICESEQ Algorithm Reports a Failure.

ports.name: HOLD
ports.description: TRUE when algorithm is waiting to execute current step.

ports.name: DONE
ports.description: TRUE When Master Completes last Defined Step. (Can be used to daisy chain multiple MASTERSEQ).

MASTERSEQ.description: The MASTERSEQ (SEQUENCER, MASTER) algorithm provides a supervisory algorithm to control sequential execution of control functions.

ports.name: CNTL
ports.description: Control Indicator Word Bit Description 0 MRE Output Type 0 = One-shot signal 1 = Maintained signal 1 High/Low Output 0 = Selected high GOOD quality output 1 = Select low GOOD quality output 2 Quality Alarm Type 0 = BAD Quality Alarm 1 = Not GOOD Quality Alarm

ports.name: XBQ
ports.description: Output (digital); all transmitters in Quality Alarm

ports.name: XABQ
ports.description: Output (digital); Quality Alarm for Transmitter A

ports.name: XBBQ
ports.description: Output (digital); Quality Alarm for Transmitter B

ports.name: XCBQ
ports.description: Output (digital); Quality Alarm for Transmitter C

ports.name: ABDC
ports.description: Output (digital); Control Deviation Alarm between Transmitters A and B

ports.name: ABDA
ports.description: Output (digital); Alarm Deviation Alarm between Transmitters A and B

ports.name: ACDC
ports.description: Output (digital); Control Deviation Alarm between Transmitter A and C

ports.name: ACDA
ports.description: Output (digital); Alarm Deviation Alarm between Transmitters A and C

ports.name: BCDC
ports.description: Output (digital); Control Deviation Alarm Transmitters B and C

ports.name: BCDA
ports.description: Output (digital); Alarm Deviation Alarm between Transmitters B and C

ports.name: PBPT
ports.description: Output (packed digital) Bit Description 0 Manual reject 1 Transmitter malfunction 2 All transmitters in Quality Alarm 3 Quality Alarm for Transmitter A 4 Quality Alarm for Transmitter B 5 Quality Alarm for Transmitter C 6 Alarm deviation between Transmitters A and B 7 Alarm deviation between Transmitters A and C 8 Alarm deviation between Transmitters B and C 9 Control deviation between Transmitters A and B 10 Control deviation between Transmitters A and C 11 Control deviation between Transmitters B and C 12 Inhibit Control Deviation Check for MRE Output 13 Transmitter A mode 14 Transmitter B mode 15 Transmitter C mode

MEDIANSEL.description: The MEDIANSEL (Median value selector, quality and deviation checks) algorithm monitors analog transmitter inputs for quality and deviation from each other.

ports.name: IN1G
ports.description: Gain on input 1. The gain on input 1 should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: TPSC
ports.description: Maximum value of the output point

ports.name: BTSC
ports.description: Minimum value of the output point

ports.name: TOUT
ports.description: Track output value mode & status signals for input 1 variable

ports.name: TRIN
ports.description: Tracking & limiting mode signals and tracking value; analog input variable

MULTIPLY.description: The MULTIPLY algorithm multiplies two gained and biased inputs.

NLOG.description: The NLOG algorithm performs the mathematical natural logarithmic function.
NLOG.formula: OUT = NATURAL LOG OF IN1 + BIAS

NOT.description: The NOT algorithm is a logical NOT gate.
NOT.formula: IF IN1 = TRUE THEN OUT = FALSE ELSE OUT = TRUE

ports.name: BASE
ports.description: Time Base in seconds (minimum 0.1 second, default 1.0 seconds)

OFFDELAY.description: The OFFDELAY algorithm extends the time that the output is TRUE.

ports.name: BASE
ports.description: Time Base seconds (minimum 0.1 second, default 1.0 seconds)

ONDELAY.description: The ONDELAY algorithm delays the time that the output will be set to TRUE.

ports.name: BASE
ports.description: Time Base in seconds (minimum 0.1 second, default 1.0 seconds); (0 implies 1.0 second)

ONESHOT.description: The ONESHOT algorithm sets the output TRUE on a transition for a specified period of time.

OR.description: The OR (Logical OR gate up to 8 inputs) algorithm changes the Boolean of the output based on the input.The output equals the logical OR of two to eight inputs (that is, at least one input must be TRUE for the output to be TRUE).

PACK16.description: The PACK16 algorithm specifies up to 16 optional digital values as inputs, which are placed into their corresponding positions in the A2 record field of an LP or larger point record.

ports.name: SPTG
ports.description: Gain on set point. The gain on the set point should never be initialized to zero.

ports.name: PVG
ports.description: Gain on process variable input. The gain on the set point should never be initialized to zero.

ports.name: TPSC
ports.description: Maximum value of the output point

ports.name: BTSC
ports.description: Minimum value of the output point

ports.name: TYPE
ports.description: Type of PID controller: NORMAL: Regular PID control ESG: PID control with error squared on the proportional gain term ESI: PID control with error squared on the integral term

ports.name: ACTN
ports.description: INDIRECT: Error = Set point - process variable DIRECT: Error = Process variable - set point

ports.name: CASC
ports.description: Controller is downstream in a cascaded configuration. NORMAL: Normal PID action. CASCADED: See description of cascaded mode and conditional tracking.

ports.name: DACT
ports.description: Type of Derivative action Normal — Derivative applied to change in error. Set point — Derivative applied to change in set point. Process — Derivative applied to change in process variable.

ports.name: PGAIN
ports.description: PID proportional gain. If the proportional gain equals zero, the proportional part will not be included in the output.

ports.name: INTG
ports.description: PID integral time in seconds per repeat. If the integral time equals zero, the integral part will not be included in the output.

ports.name: DGAIN
ports.description: PID derivative gain. If the derivative gain equals zero, the derivative part will not be included in the output.

ports.name: DRAT
ports.description: PID derivative rate decay constant in seconds

ports.name: DEVA
ports.description: Error between process variable and set point for PID. This is calculated using the normalized set point PVAR.

PID.description: The PID algorithm provides a proportional, integral, derivative controller function.

ports.name: SPTG
ports.description: Gain on set point. The gain on the set point should never be initialized to zero.

ports.name: PVG
ports.description: Gain on process variable input. The gain on the set point should never be initialized to zero.

ports.name: FFG
ports.description: Gain on Feedforward. The gain on feedforward should never be initialized to zero.

ports.name: DACT
ports.description: Type of Derivative action Normal- Derivative applied to change in error. Set point- Derivative applied to change in set point. Process- Derivative applied to change in process variable.

ports.name: TPSC
ports.description: Maximum value of the output point

ports.name: BTSC
ports.description: Minimum value of the output point

ports.name: TYPE
ports.description: Type of PID controller: NORMAL: Regular PID control ESG: PID control with error squared on the proportional gain term ESI: PID control with error squared on the integral term

ports.name: ACTN
ports.description: Direction Flag: INDIRECT: Error = Set point - process variable DIRECT: Error = Process variable - set point

ports.name: CASC
ports.description: Controller is downstream in a cascaded configuration. NORMAL: Normal PID action. CASCADED: See description of cascaded mode and conditional tracking.

ports.name: PGAIN
ports.description: PID proportional gain. If the proportional gain equals zero, the proportional part will not be included in the output.

ports.name: INTG
ports.description: PID integral time in seconds per repeat. If the integral time equals zero, the integral part will not be included in the output.

ports.name: DGAIN
ports.description: PID derivative gain. If the derivative gain equals zero, the derivative part will not be included in the output.

ports.name: DRAT
ports.description: PID derivative rate decay constant in seconds

PIDFF.description: The PIDFF algorithm is designed to accept an externally generated analog signal that is used as a feed-forward bias.

ports.name: BITA
ports.description: Which bit in the point record’s status word is output to OUTA

ports.name: BITB
ports.description: Which bit in the point record’s status word is output to OUTB

ports.name: OUTA
ports.description: When the ENBL input is TRUE, this output reflects the state of the bit specified by BITA.

ports.name: OUTB
ports.description: When the ENBL input is TRUE, this output reflects the state of the bit specified by BITB.

PNTSTATUS.description: The PNTSTATUS algorithm outputs the states of two specified bits of the point record’s status word.

ports.name: CX0
ports.description: Zero coefficient of the polynomial equation

ports.name: CX1
ports.description: First coefficient of the polynomial equation

ports.name: CX2
ports.description: Second coefficient of the polynomial equation

ports.name: CX3
ports.description: Third coefficient of the polynomial equation

ports.name: CX4
ports.description: Fourth coefficient of the polynomial equation

ports.name: CX5
ports.description: Fifth coefficient of the polynomial equation

POLYNOMIAL.description: The POLYNOMIAL algorithm performs a mathematical fifth order polynomial function.
POLYNOMIAL.formula: OUT = CX0 (IN1)0 + CX1 (IN1)1 + CX2 (IN1)2 + CX3 (IN1)3 + CX4 (IN1)4 + CX5 (IN1)5

ports.name: PVG
ports.description: Gain on process input. The gain on the input should never be initial- ized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: DLIN
ports.description: Output of the TRANSPORT algo- rithm(s)

ports.name: FOTC
ports.description: The 1st time constant in the model.

ports.name: DOUT
ports.description: Input to the downstream TRANS- PORT. Analog output variable

PREDICTOR.description: This algorithm can be utilized to implement a standard Smith Predictor control system structure in processes that are characterized by dynamics that include a deadtime.

PSLT.description: PSLT calculates Pressure of Saturated Liquid given its Temperature.

PSVS.description: PSVS calculates Pressure of Saturated Vapor given its Entropy (S).

PULSECNT.description: The PULSECNT algorithm counts the number of FALSE to TRUE transitions of the digital input point.
PULSECNT.formula: IF RSET THEN OUT = 0 IF IN1 = TRUE AND OLDIN = FALSE THEN OUT = OUT + 1 where:

QAVERAGE.description: The QAVERAGE algorithm output is the unweighted average of the N inputs.
QAVERAGE.formula: If the quality is GOOD for all points, and the points are being updated, THEN OUT = (IN1 + ...INN)/N Any point with BAD quality is excluded from the average. If all points have BAD quality, THEN OUT = OLDOUT and the quality is BAD.

ports.name: CHK
ports.description: Quality check type: BAD, FAIR, Not GOOD, GOOD

QUALITYMON.description: The QUALITYMON algorithm sets the digital output signal (OUT) TRUE if the input value (IN1) has the same quality as selected in the quality type field(X1).
QUALITYMON.formula: If the quality of IN1 is the selected quality type, or if its value is not being updated, THEN OUT = TRUE ELSE OUT = FALSE

ports.name: SMTH
ports.description: Smoothing time constant in seconds Note This is approximately 1/5 of the total time to settle. For example, for 1 minute total, set SMTH to 12 seconds.

RATECHANGE.description: The RATECHANGE algorithm computes the output analog value (OUT) as a measure of the rate of change or velocity of the smoothed input (IN1).

ports.name: RALM
ports.description: Rate of change limit in units per second

RATELIMIT.description: The RATELIMIT algorithm is a rate limiter with fixed rate limit and flag when rate limit is exceeded.
RATELIMIT.formula: PLR = RALM * (TS/1000) TEMP = (IN1 - OLDOUT) IF ABS(TEMP) < PLR THEN OUT = IN1 FOUT = FALSE ELSE IF TEMP > 0.0 THEN OUT = OLDOUT + RALM ELSE OUT = OLDOUT - RALM FOUT = TRUE where:

ports.name: PRAT
ports.description: Rate of change limit in the positive direction (absolute value)

ports.name: PDB
ports.description: Deadband on the positive rate of change limit (absolute value)

ports.name: NRAT
ports.description: Rate of change limit in the negative direction (absolute value)

ports.name: NDB
ports.description: Deadband on the negative rate of change limit (absolute value)

RATEMON.description: The RATEMON algorithm is a rate of change monitor with reset deadband and fixed/variable rate limit.
RATEMON.formula: RATE = (IN1 - OLDIN)/TS IF (RATE > PRAT) OR (RATE < (0 - NRAT)) THEN OUT = TRUE ELSE IF (RATE < (PRAT - PDB)) AND (RATE > (0 - (NRAT - NDB))) THEN OUT = FALSE where:

RESETSUM.description: The RESETSUM algorithm accumulates until told to reset.
RESETSUM.formula: IF R3 ≠ 0 THEN OUT = R3 IF RUN = TRUE THEN TEMP = OUT + (GAIN x IN1) IF FFLG = TRUE THEN FOUT = TEMP IF RSET = TRUE THEN OUT = R1 ELSE OUT = TEMP where:

ports.name: IN1
ports.description: Input, read trigger of the RPA card (digital)

RPACNT.description: The RPACNT algorithm will read the pulse count from the Ovation Pulse Accumulator card.

RPAWIDTH.description: The RPAWIDTH algorithm will read the pulse width from the Ovation Pulse Accumulator card.

ports.name: UNIT
ports.description: Units of time 0 = tenths of a second 1 = seconds 2 = minutes 3 = hours 4 = days

ports.name: NUM
ports.description: Number of samples to be averaged (1 through 8)

RUNAVERAGE.description: The RUNAVERAGE algorithm performs a running average calculation on a number of samples collected at a sampling interval time.
RUNAVERAGE.formula: The quality of the output is as follows:

RVPSTATUS.description: The RVPSTATUS algorithm performs the following: • Displays the status register and command register for the Ovation Valve Positioner (RVP) Card.

SATOSP.description: The SATOSP algorithm transfers one analog value to a packed point record for use by programmable controllers.

SELECTOR.description: The SELECTOR algorithm transfers between N analog inputs.

ports.name: PCNT
ports.description: Percent change of output in first four seconds

ports.name: TIME
ports.description: Number of seconds remaining for ramp to full scale

ports.name: CARD
ports.description: Card type: SOFT = No hardware interface RLI = Ovation Loop Interface card

ports.name: TRIN
ports.description: Tracking & limiting mode signals and tracking value; analog input variable

SETPOINT.description: The SETPOINT algorithm performs a manual loader function.

SINE.description: The SINE algorithm performs a mathematical sine function.

SLCAIN.description: The SLCAIN algorithm reads up to 16 analog values from a Group 1 QLC or Ovation Link Controller (LC) card (or redundant pair of Group 1 QLC cards).

SLCAOUT.description: The SLCAOUT algorithm writes up to 16 analog points to a Group 1 QLC or LC card (or redundant pair of Group 1 QLC cards).

SLCDIN.description: The SLCDIN algorithm reads up to 16 digital values from a Group 1 QLC or Ovation Link Controller (LC) card (or redundant pair of Group 1 QLC cards).

SLCDOUT.description: The SLCDOUT algorithm writes up to 16 digital points to a Group 1 QLC or Ovation Link Controller (LC) card (or redundant pair of Group 1 QLC cards).

SLCPIN.description: The SLCPIN algorithm reads up to 16 packed points from a Group1 QLC card or LC (or redundant pair of Group 1 QLC cards).

SLCPOUT.description: The SLCPOUT algorithm writes up to 16 packed points to a Group 1 QLC card or LC (or redundant pair of Group 1 QLC cards).

ports.name: PAUX
ports.description: Primary QLC/ LC auxiliary fault information (packed)

ports.name: PSTA
ports.description: Primary QLC/ LC hardware status information (packed)

ports.name: SAUX
ports.description: Secondary QLC/ LC auxiliary fault information (packed)

ports.name: SSTA
ports.description: Secondary QLC/ LC hardware status information (packed)

SLCSTATUS.description: The SLCSTATUS algorithm reads hardware and user application status information from a Group 1 QLC or Ovation Link Controller (LC) card (or redundant pair of Group 1 QLC cards).

SMOOTH.description: This algorithm “smooths” an analog input valve.
SMOOTH.formula: OUT = (alpha x IN1) + (beta x oldout) where:

SPTOSA.description: The SPTOSA algorithm transfers a packed point record into an analog point record.

ports.name: IN1G
ports.description: Gain on input. The gain on the input should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: TPSC
ports.description: Maximum value of the output point

ports.name: BTSC
ports.description: Minimum value of the output point

ports.name: TOUT
ports.description: Track output value, mode & status signal for Input 1 variable

ports.name: TRIN
ports.description: Tracking & limiting mode signals & tracking value; analog input variable

SQUAREROOT.description: The SQUAREROOT algorithm multiplies the analog input with an internal gain, adds a bias and then takes the square root.
SQUAREROOT.formula: IN1GB = (IN1 x IN1 GAIN) + IN1 BIAS IF IN1GB > 0 THEN OUT = SQUARE ROOT OF IN1GB ELSE OUT = 0 IF OUT ≥ TPSC THEN OUT = TPSC ELSE IF OUT ≤ BTSC THEN OUT = BTSC

SSLT.description: SSLT calculates Entropy (S) of Saturated Liquid given its Temperature.

ports.name: IN1
ports.description: Analog Flow Transmitter Delta Pressure Input

STEAMFLOW.description: The STEAMFLOW algorithm performs a flow compensation based on a flow measurement on an input as a differential pressure input or a flow input (IN1).

ports.name: UNIT
ports.description: EngineeringUnits(0-English (Default), 1 - SI) See Engineering Units Table below.

STEAMTABLE.description: The STEAMTABLE algorithm calculates thermodynamic properties of water and steam.

ports.name: TRIN
ports.description: Input (analog); tracks the step to this value

ports.name: UNIT
ports.description: Code number for the units of time to be used. Value Units of Time 0 0.1 seconds (default) 1 seconds 2 minutes 3 hours

ports.name: RHRS
ports.description: Output (analog); the number of hours for the time remaining in the current timing cycle

ports.name: RMIN
ports.description: Output (analog); the number of minutes for the time remaining in the current timing cycle

ports.name: RSEC
ports.description: Output (analog); the number of seconds for the time remaining in the current timing cycle

ports.name: EHRS
ports.description: Output (analog); the number of hours for the time elapsed in the current timing cycle

ports.name: EMIN
ports.description: Output (analog); the number of minutes for the time elapsed in the current timing cycle

ports.name: ESEC
ports.description: Output (analog); the number of seconds for the time elapsed in the current timing cycle

STEPTIME.description: The STEPTIME algorithm is an automatic step timer.

ports.name: IN1G
ports.description: Gain on input 1. The gain on the input 1 should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: IN1B
ports.description: Bias on input 1. The gain on the input 1 should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: IN2G
ports.description: Gain on input 2. The gain on the input 1 should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: IN2B
ports.description: Bias on input 2. The gain on the input 1 should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: IN3G
ports.description: Gain on Input 3. The gain on the input 1 should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: IN3B
ports.description: Bias on Input 3. The gain on the input 1 should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: IN4G
ports.description: Gain on Input 4. The gain on the input 1 should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: TPSC
ports.description: Maximum value of the output point

ports.name: BTSC
ports.description: Minimum value of the output point

ports.name: TRIN
ports.description: Tracking & Limiting mode signals & tracking value; analog input variable

ports.name: TOUT
ports.description: Track output value, mode & status signal for input 1 variable

SUM.description: The output of the SUM algorithm is the sum of the four individually gained and biased inputs.

SYSTEMTIME.description: While the RUN flag is set, the SYSTEMTIME algorithm accesses the time from the Contoller’s time (expressed in Universal Time Coordinates (UTC)) and stores it in the separate, optional, analog outputs for seconds, minutes, hours, day, month, and year.

TANGENT.description: The TANGENT algorithm performs a mathematical tangent function.

TIMECHANGE.description: The TIMECHANGE algorithm checks the Controller’s time (expressed in Universal Time Coordinates (UTC)) against the old values of the time.

ports.name: WEEK
ports.description: Day of the week (Day range. For example, 1 = Sunday)

TIMEDETECT.description: The TIMEDETECT algorithm checks the hour value from the Controller’s time (expressed in Universal Time Coordinates (UTC)) against the old hour value for a change.

TIMEMON.description: If the RUN flag is set, the TIMEMON algorithm provides the following functions: • If the controller Date/Time is between the Date 1/Time 1 and Date 2/Time 2 tuning constants, the optional digital output flag, FLG1 is set TRUE.

ports.name: IN1G
ports.description: Gain on input1. The gain on the input1 should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: IN2G
ports.description: Gain on input2. The gain on the input2 should never be initialized to zero; if it is, the drop is placed into alarm.

ports.name: TPSC
ports.description: Maximum value of the output point

ports.name: BTSC
ports.description: Minimum value of the output point

ports.name: SLEW
ports.description: Internal tracking option: OFF:No tracking during a transfer. ON:Tracking is implemented during a transfer.

ports.name: TRR1
ports.description: Tracking ramp rate from input1 to input2 or from the track input to the input2 (units per second)

ports.name: TRR2
ports.description: Tracking ramp rate from input2 to input1or from the track input to the input1 (units per second)

ports.name: OTRK
ports.description: Output Tracking Option: OFF=output value does not track. ON=output value tracks.

ports.name: FLAG
ports.description: Digital input signal to select output (required); the user must enter the name of a point.

ports.name: TRK2
ports.description: Track output value, node & status signals for Input 2 variable

ports.name: TRK1
ports.description: Track output value mode & status signals for input 1 variable

ports.name: TRIN
ports.description: Tracking & limiting mode signals and tracking value; analog input variable

TRANSFER.description: The TRANSFER algorithm performs a transfer between the two inputs.

TRANSLATOR.description: The TRANSLATOR algorithm translates the output based on the input of a predefined table.

ports.name: INIT
ports.description: Sample initialization 0 or 1 = Samples are initialized to zero when the algorithm is first added to the drop; samples are not changed at power-up, reset, or failover, and may contain old values. 2 = Samples are initialized to the current value of the IN1 analog input on power-up, reset, and failover (as well as when the algorithm is first added to the drop). 3 = Samples are initialized to the current value of the OUT analog output on power-up, reset, and failover (as well as when the algorithm is first added to the drop). 4 = Samples are initialized to zero on power-up, reset, and failover (as well as when the algorithm is first added to the drop).

TRANSPORT.description: The TRANSPORT algorithm samples the analog input point and outputs the sample value with a time delay.

TRNSFNDX.description: The TRNSFNDX algorithm will select the output analog value from up to 64 outputs which hold the input IN1.

TSLH.description: TSLH calculates Temperature for Saturated Liquid given its Enthalpy (H).

TSLP.description: TSLP calculates Saturation Temperature of Saturated Liquid given its Pressure.

UNPACK16.description: The UNPACK16 algorithm specifies up to 16 optional, packed digital values in the A2 record field of a packed LP point record as optional outputs of this algorithm.

VCLTP.description: VCLTP calculates Specific Volume of Compressed Liquid given its Temperature and Pressure.

ports.name: VOLUME
ports.description: Specific Volume of Saturated Liquid output

VSLT.description: VSLT calculates Specific Volume of Saturated Liquid given its Temperature.

ports.name: IN2
ports.description: Analog Input - this is the positive feedback from the device.

ports.name: IN1
ports.description: Analog Input in percent (0-100). This is the demand value for the device.

ports.name: TOUT
ports.description: Bit 16 is set TRUE during the first pass and when DEVO input is TRUE. Typically used for initial- izing upsteam PID algorithm to accomplish bumpless transfer. Bit 19 and 30 indicate POS is at low limit Bit 20 and 31 indicate POS is at high limit. Contains the value of the position feedback signal. The quality of TOUT is always GOOD; sensor errors are not reported. Used to initialize upsteam PID algorithms with bumpless transfer.

ports.name: DEVO
ports.description: Packed Point; Bit 0 indicates the operational state of the device being controlled, where: 0 = operational 1 = non-operational

ports.name: OUT
ports.description: Contains the value of the position feedback signal. The quality and IN field of OUT are used to report error conditions.

ports.name: TRIN
ports.description: Tracking and limiting mode. Signals, tracking value, analog input variable.

X3STEP.description: The X3STEP algorithm controls devices that must be kept within a certain tolerance and tuned by an operator.

XOR.description: The XOR algorithm performs a mathematical exclusive OR function.
XOR.formula: IF IN1 = IN2 THEN OUT = FALSE ELSE OUT = TRUE
